title: What is the difference between sleep() and wait() in Java?

answers.junior: sleep() pauses a thread for a set time. wait() makes a thread wait until notified.
answers.middle: sleep() is for timing, does not release locks. wait() is for synchronization, releases the lock and waits for notify().
answers.senior: sleep() is static, used for delays, does not require synchronization. wait() is instance, used in synchronized blocks, releases monitor and waits for notify/notifyAll. Incorrect use of wait() outside sync causes errors.
answers.tricky: Use wait() for inter-thread communication, sleep() for timing. Avoid using sleep() for synchronization. Handle InterruptedException properly. Producer-consumer patterns rely on wait/notify.
